title: May December
logline: An actress researches a controversial woman who had an affair with a minor decades earlier for an upcoming film role, gradually becoming dangerously absorbed in her subject's life and identity.
antagonistOrConflict: The blurred boundaries between performance and reality as Elizabeth becomes increasingly consumed by Gracie's identity
tone: Quietly unsettling, psychologically complex, with mounting tension beneath a polite Southern veneer
themes: The nature of truth and perception
The cost of fame and notoriety

actBreaks.description: Elizabeth's initial research phase ends with intimate dinner at Atherton-Yoo house, establishing all key relationships

validation.ruling: Scene #23 establishes the central dynamic through the tense dinner where Gracie's controlling nature is fully revealed through her treatment of Charlie and the family hierarchy, setting up the psychological investigation that will drive Act 2.

actBreaks.description: Family dinner at Garibaldi's marks peak of Elizabeth's integration into the family before psychological unraveling begins

validation.ruling: Scene #68 functions as a gathering of all characters for a celebratory dinner that masks underlying tensions, serving as the calm before the storm of revelations and confrontations that will comprise the final act.

actBreaks.description: Elizabeth's complete transformation culminates in final soundstage scenes

validation.ruling: Scene #87 serves as the true ending, showing Elizabeth desperately trying to perfect her portrayal of Gracie on set, having become psychologically consumed by the role. This meta-theatrical conclusion reveals how deeply the experience has affected her.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: Character study with mounting psychological tension, following Elizabeth's gradual absorption into Gracie's world and identity
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: Deliberately paced with short, observational scenes averaging 1.4 pages. Tension builds through accumulation of small moments rather than dramatic beats. Act 1 establishes relationships (scenes 1-23), Act 2 deepens psychological entanglement (scenes 24-68), Act 3 reveals complete transformation (scenes 69-87).

incitingIncident.description: Joe Yoo, 36, tends to a grill in the lush backyard of the Atherton-Yoo house on Tybee Island, described as handsome but melancholy like a Dutch painting figure.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE - BACKYARD - TYBEE ISLAND - DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #6 is purely atmospheric setup introducing Joe's character without creating forward momentum or conflict. The actual inciting incident likely occurs later when Elizabeth and the central characters first interact meaningfully, but no strong candidate appears in the given options within the 9-24pp window.

midpoint.description: Elizabeth interviews Morris Sperber, Gracie's former defense attorney, who explains how defending her became his most famous case and describes Gracie's compelling, childlike quality.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. CRAB SHACK RESTAURANT - TYBEE ISLAND-- DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #41 perfectly positioned at 42.6% serves as the true midpoint revelation where Morris reveals Gracie's criminal past and her appearance in the New York Times. This exposes the dark truth Elizabeth has been researching and fundamentally shifts her understanding of who Gracie really is.

climax.description: Gracie, dressed in white and wearing dark glasses, strides confidently through the graduation crowd despite whispers. She encounters Elizabeth, also in white, who now carries equal confidence. Elizabeth announces she's leaving for the airport.
climax.sceneHeading: EXT. FOOTBALL FIELD-- A BIT LATER

validation.ruling: Scene #86 at 97.9% delivers the climactic confrontation between Elizabeth and Gracie at graduation. Elizabeth has gained confidence and understanding, while Gracie maintains her manipulative control, denying Georgie's revelations and asserting her dominance one final time before Elizabeth's departure.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE OUTDOORS-- DAWN
scenes.summary: Opening with urgent music as a monarch butterfly lays white eggs on milkweed leaves, establishing the life cycle metaphor that will run throughout the film.

scenes.heading: EXT. HISTORIC DISTRICT - SAVANNAH, GEORGIA-- DAY
scenes.summary: Memorial Day 2015 in historic Savannah, Georgia - a picturesque Southern setting with American flags and a marching band assembling.

scenes.heading: EXT. INN - HISTORIC SAVANNAH-- DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth Berry, 36, arrives at a Victorian inn, unpacking her luggage from a rental car in the historic district.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth reorganizes her inn room while on a phone call, discovering a welcome gift basket noting the staff are fans of her show 'Animal Hospital.'

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth sits cross-legged on the bed with a new notebook, writing 'Gracie' and underlining it as she begins her character research.

scenes.heading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE - BACKYARD - TYBEE ISLAND - DAY
scenes.summary: Joe Yoo, 36, tends to a grill in the lush backyard of the Atherton-Yoo house on Tybee Island, described as handsome but melancholy like a Dutch painting figure.
scenes.turningPointNote: First introduction to Joe, establishing his melancholic nature and the domestic setting where much of the drama will unfold

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE - KITCHEN-- DAY
scenes.summary: In the kitchen, Gracie Atherton-Yoo, 59, prepares desserts with friend Rhonda while Joe gets a beer. Gracie monitors Joe's drinking and mentions meeting Judge Judy.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: At the backyard barbecue, Joe talks with friend Ben who admits to looking up 'Elizabeth Berry naked' online while kids play around them.

scenes.heading: EXT. CATALINA DRIVE--DAY
scenes.summary: POV shot through windshield driving past Spanish moss-draped oaks and marsh landscape toward 1970s-80s suburban homes.

scenes.heading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE-- DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth arrives at the coral pink Atherton-Yoo house carrying wine, finds no one answers the front door, picks up a brown package and heads toward backyard sounds.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Elizabeth enters the Memorial Day barbecue in full swing, glowing in sunlight as Joe spots her first, then she locks eyes with Gracie who greets her warmly.
scenes.turningPointNote: First meeting between Elizabeth and Gracie - the central relationship that will drive the psychological drama

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gracie casually shows Elizabeth that the package contains excrement, explaining they regularly receive such hate mail from 'sickos' with practiced routine.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth wanders the party taking notes, observing Gracie's maternal authority as she directs Charlie out of the empty pool, then meets Mary and her friends.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD-- LATER AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: As the party winds down, Elizabeth watches Gracie cuddled in Joe's arms while Rhonda comments on how beloved the couple is in their community.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elizabeth in bed studying 1992 People Magazine with young Gracie in handcuffs on the cover, headlines about the 'pet shop romance' with her 13-year-old lover.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elizabeth diving into the scandalous history, establishing the magnitude of what happened between Gracie and young Joe

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO DEN-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe researches Elizabeth online at the family computer, watching her interviews, award shows, and commercials with low volume in the dark den.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO BEDROOM-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe gets into bed smelling of smoke, leading to a tense exchange with Gracie about his hygiene that ends with her crying as he apologizes and holds her.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD-- EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Early morning ritual of Joe carefully examining milkweed leaves for monarch eggs, delicately clipping them and placing them on a tray.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe transfers the egg-laden leaves into white mesh cages in the living room, photographing a perfectly aligned set of three eggs to text to his monarch enthusiast group.

scenes.heading: EXT. COMMUNITY CENTER-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Elizabeth arrives at an unglamorous community center building in the afternoon.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMUNITY CENTER-- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth approaches a flower-arranging class where Gracie spots her through glass doors and waves her in warmly.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMUNITY CENTER-- LATER
scenes.summary: Gracie teaches Elizabeth formal flower arranging in threes while discussing Elizabeth's engagement ring and wedding plans, with Gracie taking over Elizabeth's more natural arrangement.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO KITCHEN-- DAY
scenes.summary: Intimate family dinner with Elizabeth as guest, Gracie serving quail and controlling Charlie's milk consumption while he resists, Joe remaining passive as Gracie dominates.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elizabeth witnesses Gracie's controlling maternal dynamics firsthand, seeing the family's complex power structure

scenes.heading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE-- DUSK
scenes.summary: Outside after dinner, Elizabeth asks Joe about visiting his work, leading to an awkward conversation where she realizes they're the same age despite his kids being nearly college-bound.

scenes.heading: INT. INN - BATHROOM-- MORNING
scenes.summary: Elizabeth applies makeup while talking to fiancé Aaron on speakerphone about Gracie's lack of guilt or shame, with Aaron suggesting personality disorder.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHIPPEWA SQUARE - HISTORIC SAVANNAH - DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth hurries through historic Chippewa Square past an old Savannah tour, checking an address on a piece of paper.

scenes.heading: INT. GALLERY ESPRESSO - DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth meets with Tom Atherton, Gracie's ex-husband, in a '90s-style coffee shop where he's willing to discuss the past and seems to find talking therapeutic.
scenes.turningPointNote: Meeting with Gracie's ex-husband provides crucial backstory and another perspective on the scandal

scenes.heading: INT. THE POSH LOFT - HISTORIC SAVANNAH-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Shopping for Mary's graduation dress, Gracie controls the selection while Elizabeth observes, with Gracie revealing she knows about Elizabeth's meeting with Tom.

scenes.heading: EXT. PET STORE-- DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth photographs the exterior of Telfair Pets, the site where the original relationship began between Gracie and young Joe.

scenes.heading: INT. PET STORE-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Pet store owner Mr. Henderson gives Elizabeth a tour of the closing shop, explaining how his declining mother hired loyal customer Gracie as help.

scenes.heading: INT. STOCKROOM-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth explores the dim stockroom where the relationship began, finding hiding spots and dark corners among the fish tanks that glow in the darkness.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK OF THE STOCKROOM-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: In the stockroom's back area, Elizabeth lies on wooden stairs near a refrigerator full of frozen mice, recreating and imagining the sexual encounters, covering her mouth as if to muffle screams.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elizabeth physically inhabiting the space of the original abuse, blurring boundaries between research and disturbing recreation

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM-- DAY
scenes.summary: The monarch eggs in Joe's living room cages have hatched into thick green caterpillars that writhe and eat in what's described as a 'hedonist swell.'

scenes.heading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE - POOL-- DAY
scenes.summary: Construction workers resume work on the Atherton-Yoo family pool, visible through the living room window.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- DAY
scenes.summary: Joe looks through family photos and discovers an old letter in feminine handwriting hidden in a Karate Kid photo book, reading a few lines before quickly putting it away.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Joe texts with Michela from his monarch group about her bad day, becoming fixated on her mention of taking a bath before being interrupted by Gracie.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN-- MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: In the kitchen, Gracie makes layer cakes while asking Joe about graduation logistics and warning him not to keep his caterpillars in their bedroom again.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elizabeth examines a photo of glowing new mother Gracie holding baby Honor, discovering when she unfolds the bottom that Gracie's ankle is handcuffed to the hospital bed.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth uses her asthma inhaler while reviewing audition tapes of 13-year-old boy actors auditioning to play young Joe, disturbed by how young they look.

scenes.heading: INT. X-RAY ROOM-- DAY
scenes.summary: In the dark x-ray room, Elizabeth and Joe examine slides of a 13-year-old's broken arm, with Elizabeth commenting on how small children's hands are, creating an intimate moment between them.

scenes.heading: INT. CRAB SHACK RESTAURANT - TYBEE ISLAND-- DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth interviews Morris Sperber, Gracie's former defense attorney, who explains how defending her became his most famous case and describes Gracie's compelling, childlike quality.
scenes.turningPointNote: Key exposition about the legal case and Gracie's manipulative charm from her own lawyer's perspective

scenes.heading: EXT. JOE SR.’S APARTMENT-- DAY
scenes.summary: Joe visits his father's apartment building, carrying groceries across the manicured lawn of the modest but nice two-story complex.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE SR.’S APARTMENT-- DAY
scenes.summary: Joe helps his elderly father Joe Sr. with the sliding door in his sparse apartment decorated with a memorial portrait of Joe's deceased white mother.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOE SR.’S BALCONY-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: On the balcony with a view of water, Joe Sr. chain-smokes while discussing the twins' graduation and upcoming empty nest, with subtle tension about Joe's future.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH SCHOOL-- DAY
scenes.summary: Elizabeth walks in slow-motion through a high school hallway between classes, drawing stares from young male students including a freshman who tries to impress her.

scenes.heading: INT. BLACK BOX THEATER-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: In the high school's black box theater, Elizabeth speaks to drama students about acting in sex scenes, describing the mechanical choreography and psychological complexity with unusual candor.

scenes.heading: EXT. ELIZABETH’S CAR-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: High wide-angle shot of Elizabeth's car heading toward the Atherton-Yoo house in the afternoon.

scenes.heading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Elizabeth gives Mary a ride home, with Mary appearing unwell and quickly rushing inside and slamming the door, leaving Elizabeth following slowly behind.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO BATHROOM-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth, wearing Gracie's makeup, examines Joe's caterpillars with subtle seductive energy. Joe explains his monarch butterfly conservation work, mentioning how Gracie makes it seem like his thing alone.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEIGHBORHOOD-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe and Elizabeth walk through the marshy neighborhood. Joe expresses concern about how she'll represent their story, fearing being portrayed as a victim. Elizabeth promises to try for accuracy.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE-- DUSK
scenes.summary: Joe returns home to find the house dark and silent. He immediately senses something is wrong and drops his head in frustration.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe finds Gracie silently weeping in their bedroom. She reveals that Joyce Mercer cancelled her cake orders and they're leaving town, another consequence of the scandal.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gracie presents Joe with a large three-layer coconut cake, the cancelled order. She watches expectantly as he eats and compliments it, both sitting in tense silence.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- MORNING
scenes.summary: Elizabeth speaks with Roberto, the Italian director, while looking up Georgia's Sex Offender Registry. He pressures her about budget overruns, but she convinces him she's close to finding something true.

scenes.heading: EXT. AIRPORT-- DAY
scenes.summary: Honor Atherton-Yoo, 22, with bleached hair and tired expression, arrives at the airport. Joe and Mary greet her warmly as he loads her suitcases.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: High angle shot of Joe's car driving from Savannah toward Tybee, with family conversation about Honor's roommate situation heard in voiceover.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: In the car, Honor asks about their mother and Elizabeth. Mary makes a mocking gesture about Elizabeth that makes the girls laugh, leaving Joe confused but smiling.

scenes.heading: INT. INN - TV SCREEN-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Elizabeth watches a pixelated DVD of 'The Gracie Atherton Story,' a poorly made TV movie. The actress portrays Gracie as more Mrs. Robinson than the real person - composed and in charge.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Joe sits in his den texting with Michela, discussing their mutual exhaustion and work troubles. The TV movie continues playing, showing the seduction scene in the pet shop.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe continues texting Michela, impulsively suggesting a vacation to a butterfly sanctuary in Mexico. When she reminds him he's married, he apologizes and hits his forehead in regret.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Elizabeth gets dressed while applying Gracie's makeup technique, with the TV movie's seduction scene playing in the background showing 'Gracie' removing her cardigan.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF-- LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Joe lies on the roof lost in painful thoughts. Charlie joins him and they discuss college and leaving home. Charlie admits he can't wait to leave, which stings Joe deeply.

scenes.heading: INT. FRONT HALL-- LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Elizabeth arrives early for dinner wearing Gracie's makeup style. Gracie, still in day clothes with a flour-covered apron, throws an apron at Elizabeth somewhat forcefully.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN-- A FEW MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Elizabeth helps Gracie make caramel, carefully watching temperamental browning sugar. Gracie guides her through the precise, quick process of adding butter and cream, then arranging cherries in the caramel.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE ROOF-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe has an emotional moment with Charlie on the roof. Gracie and Elizabeth appear below in matching aprons looking like twins. Joe seems stunned seeing double, nearly loses balance at the roof's edge, then breaks into hysterical laughter.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joe's psychological break seeing Elizabeth transformed into Gracie's double

scenes.heading: INT. GARIBALDI’S - HISTORIC SAVANNAH-- EARLY EVENING
scenes.summary: The family dines at Garibaldi's restaurant. Gracie gives a toast celebrating the twins' graduation and their family, including Elizabeth. Honor pointedly reminds her to include Elizabeth, creating tension.

scenes.heading: INT. GARIBALDI’S - LADIES’ ROOM-- LATER
scenes.summary: In the restaurant bathroom, Gracie confides to Elizabeth about her brother's philosophy of keeping low expectations. She reveals her hopes for the evening and her children's love were disappointed.

scenes.heading: EXT. GARIBALDI’S - HISTORIC SAVANNAH-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Outside the restaurant, Georgie approaches Elizabeth with his theory about childhood trauma. When pressed, he suggests Joe was abused, calling it 'the big one' and implying it explains everything.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Joe drives Elizabeth back to the inn. She mentions outstaying her welcome, which he doesn't contradict. She looks at him admiringly and uses her inhaler, prompting him to mention his sister's asthma.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: In Elizabeth's inn room, Joe helps her with her nebulizer treatment. The medical ritual takes on odd sensuality. He gives her a saved love letter in a pink envelope, then they share a small kiss.
scenes.turningPointNote: Physical intimacy between Elizabeth and Joe crosses a crucial boundary

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO BATHROOM-- LATER
scenes.summary: Joe steps out of the shower at home, stops and stares at himself in the bathroom mirror, confronting his reflection after the encounter with Elizabeth.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO BEDROOM-- LATER
scenes.summary: In the middle of the night, Joe sits watching Gracie sleep. When she wakes, he attempts to discuss their relationship's origins, struggling for composure as she tenses up and asks if Elizabeth prompted this.
scenes.turningPointNote: Joe finally confronts the truth about his relationship with Gracie

scenes.heading: EXT. ATHERTON-YOO HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Atherton-Yoo house sits dark against a shimmer of black trees in the night.

scenes.heading: INT. INN-- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elizabeth sits before a mirror completely transformed, channeling Gracie's voice and mannerisms. She practices what appears to be a love letter monologue about paths leading to their forbidden road despite the cost.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- DAWN
scenes.summary: At dawn, monarch chrysalides hang in golden-rimmed pods. One begins to hatch as Gracie moves through the house packing. A butterfly emerges, leaving its clear shell like dead skin.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Gracie, in full hunting gear with rifle, heads into the woods with her spaniels at sunrise, whistling as they follow her.

scenes.heading: INT. ATHERTON-YOO BEDROOM-- MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe wakes alone in bright, hot yellow light, still in his clothes on top of the blankets. Gracie is gone. He sits up and sighs, taking in the empty room.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Joe prepares pancake breakfast for five in the empty kitchen. As he sets plates, he notices movement in the butterfly cages in the den.

scenes.heading: INT. DEN-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe discovers the hatched butterfly fluttering among the green pods. He opens the cage and lets the butterfly perch on his finger, greeting it gently.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN-- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Joe carefully walks to the kitchen window with the butterfly on his hand, opens it, and releases the creature into the wind. Mary appears in her white graduation dress, asking for coffee instead of breakfast.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS-- DAY
scenes.summary: In the woods, Gracie and her dogs encounter a rabid red fox that slinks toward her hissing and twitching. She stares back with blank expression that gives way to overwhelming sympathy and despair.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE’S CAR-- MORNING
scenes.summary: Joe drops his three children at the high school for graduation. Charlie and Mary wear bright red robes. He watches them adoringly as they walk to the entrance and fold into groups of classmates.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOOTBALL FIELD-- DAY
scenes.summary: At the football field graduation ceremony, students wear blood-red robes. The valedictorian gives a speech about smooth sailing and bright futures while the principal prepares to call names for diplomas.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOOTBALL FIELD-- A BIT LATER
scenes.summary: Gracie, dressed in white and wearing dark glasses, strides confidently through the graduation crowd despite whispers. She encounters Elizabeth, also in white, who now carries equal confidence. Elizabeth announces she's leaving for the airport.
scenes.turningPointNote: Final confrontation between Gracie and Elizabeth as equals

scenes.heading: INT. SOUNDSTAGE-- DAY OR NIGHT
scenes.summary: On a film soundstage, Elizabeth repeatedly performs a scene as Gracie with a young actor as Joe, handling a snake in a pet shop stockroom replica. She pleads on her knees after multiple takes, desperate to continue the role.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elizabeth's complete psychological absorption into Gracie's identity revealed as performance
